Map-pack share-of-voice % per competitor: compute appearances/checked-slots server-side in the report assembly and add a field to LocalSeoRepeatCompetitor.
serverNeeds.evidence: totalAppearances + winsAgainstClient exist on LocalSeoRepeatCompetitor (shared/types/local-seo.ts:537-544) and checkedCount on trend points (local-seo.ts:560-564); no share % field or computation

openQuestions.question: OQ1: build the paid 49-point geo-grid scanner or ship v1 on existing single-point market data?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Defer geo-grid (DEF-* row); ship markets-x-keywords posture grid on real data. Fabricating grid data violates a hard floor.

openQuestions.question: OQ2: GBP Performance metrics (views/calls/directions) in rebuild scope?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Cut from hero card until sn-local-presence-2 ships; show rating + review count + completeness (all real).

openQuestions.question: OQ3: where do HEAD Visibility-tab capabilities (posture grid, degraded warnings, trends, Track action, manual refresh) live in the new IA?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep a Visibility lens restyled with F3 primitives — the additive-parity floor per the doc's own trade-off table.

openQuestions.question: OQ4: reinstate the Verified badge the prototype shows?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep suppressed — provider cannot reliably report unclaimed listings (shipped correctness decision, GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:44-47).

openQuestions.question: OQ5: where do the authenticated review-sync trigger, per-location sync health, and copy-policy guidance live in the new Reviews pipeline?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Pipeline header block: Sync button + per-location status chips + policy footer; failures stay visible.

openQuestions.question: OQ6: keep manual Refresh buttons (incl GBP bootstrap path) or move to scheduled refresh?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep both manual triggers; scheduled refresh is a separate cron-registry decision. Bootstrap chicken-and-egg was a real shipped bug.

openQuestions.question: OQ7: declined/cancelled renderings + Reopen-for-edits. NEWLY RESOLVED HALF: approved→draft is confirmed ILLEGAL today (state-machines.ts:176) — so N10 requires a deliberate state-machine change, not just UI.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Render declined/cancelled in the funnel (mandatory); defer Reopen (sn-local-presence-7) to a follow-up with its own sign-off.

openQuestions.question: OQ8: prototype 2 sub-tabs vs HEAD 4 deep-linked tabs — redirect/alias plan for existing ?tab= bookmarks.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep 4 receiver-recognized values; if lenses collapse to 3, alias overview→presence per D8 redirect-map requirement.

openQuestions.question: Are the four flags (local-gbp, gbp-auth-connection, gbp-auth-reviews, gbp-review-responses) retired during this surface rebuild or kept?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep all four — local-gbp is a paid/tier gate and the gbp-* flags gate backend phases, not UI shell; retire nothing without the flag-lifecycle process.

kitNewFeatures.rationale: No data source exists; full build is a new paid scan job + table (sn-local-presence-1). Quick-win posture grid ships instead.

kitNewFeatures.proposal: N2 profile views + calls/directions hero metrics
kitNewFeatures.rationale: Requires new Google scope + ingestion (sn-local-presence-2); rendering them now would fabricate numbers.
Directly contradicts a shipped correctness decision — provider defaults is_claimed=true, so the badge would lie.

kitNewFeatures.proposal: N4 extended profile-health checklist with Fix flows
kitNewFeatures.rationale: Adopt the checklist PRESENTATION over the 3 real completeness signals; full data ingestion (sn-local-presence-8) deferred with a DEF-* row.
Derivable server-side from existing snapshots (sn-local-presence-3, S); doc calls it the cheapest new item.
Pure re-presentation of existing statuses; must add declined/cancelled renderings the prototype omitted.
Needs a new notification endpoint (sn-local-presence-4); product/workflow change per the D4-Q4 send-boundary posture.
No URL persisted today (sn-local-presence-5); needs sync-time capture verification against the Google API payload first.
AI re-draft already works via the existing upsert (google-business-profile.ts:303); manual-create + draft-and-send are small additive routes (sn-local-presence-6).
approved→draft is illegal in the state machine today (state-machines.ts:176); a transition-table change needs its own sign-off (sn-local-presence-7).

Visibility (posture grid, trends, repeat competitors + Track, manual Refresh)
Reviews (funnel + desk filters + response pipeline + auth sync block)

composition.statesPlan: Every lens: Skeleton loading, not-configured EmptyState with Open-setup CTA (row 7), first-load ErrorState with retry rendered BEFORE the flag check (row 13), flag-OFF renders nothing via empty server payload (row 31), 403 tier / 404 flag / 409 conflict surfaced as error bands not swallowed (row 48), connection-gated empties for auth reviews (row 35).

D3: Page Intelligence moves into SEO Editor Research mode — the mode="page" annotation mount follows it

Client Inbox surface: gbp_review_response deliverable renderer is the client half of send-to-client (row 39)

Per-node DataForSEO cost for a 49-point geo-grid scan (pricing, budget-gate design) — external provider question
Whether KeywordHub's LocalPresenceHandoff card survives the Keywords pilot rebuild unchanged — owned by that surface's ledger, not checked here

verdicts.evidence: server/db/migrations/096-local-seo-visibility.sql:31-56 defines local_visibility_snapshots as one row per (keyword, market_id) — market_id FKs local_seo_markets, no lat/lng grid-node concept; 099-client-locations.sql only adds match/location columns. rg for geogrid|geo_grid|geoGrid|grid.?scan|ScanNode across server/ shared/ src/ returned zero hits. No geo-grid job, table, or read projection exists.

server/google-business-profile-client.ts exports only exchangeGbpOAuthCode, getValidGbpAccessToken, listGbpAccountsFromGoogle (:276), listGbpLocationsFromGoogle (:296), listGbpReviewsFromGoogle (:349), updateGbpReviewReply (:386), sync/disconnect. GBP_SCOPE is a single business-manage scope; no performance/insights/impressions/profileViews/callClicks/directions method or scope anywhere in server/. Ingestion + snapshot + hero-card serialization all absent.

shared/types/local-seo.ts:537-544 LocalSeoRepeatCompetitor has totalAppearances + winsAgainstClient + markets + suggestedTrackingKeywords but NO share/percentage field. getLocalSeoCompetitorBrands (server/domains/local-seo/visibility-read-model.ts:28-100) is a pure in-memory reduction over snapshots — no writes, workspace-scoped, safe for a hot read path — and already accumulates totalAppearances per competitor and iterates every checked snapshot row, so a share-of-voice %% is a small added compute in this exact function plus one field on the computed (not DB-stored) type. No migration/mapper/serialization lockstep needed; effort S stands.

A GENERIC client-nudge path already exists and already covers GBP awaiting_client review responses. server/routes/deliverables.ts:275-299 POST /api/deliverables/:workspaceId/:id/remind → remindDeliverable() (server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts:299-313) sends the client email reminder (notifyClientOfReminder), broadcasts DELIVERABLE_UPDATED, and the route logs deliverable_reminded activity. GBP review-response sends go through sendToClient() creating a client_deliverable in awaiting_client (server/routes/google-business-profile.ts:365-386), so that deliverable is ALREADY remindable via this endpoint. The claim's 'grep in google-business-profile.ts found no nudge' is a naming-drift miss — the reuse lives under the deliverables domain the claim itself flagged in likelySharedWith. So the notification/email + activity-log halves EXIST and are reusable; the only genuinely-new work is a rate-limit/throttle on remind (none exists today). Kind should be 'reuse existing generic remind path (+ optional rate-limit)', not a net-new endpoint; effort drops from M toward S.
verdicts.correction: existsToday should be 'yes-reusable' for the nudge machinery: POST /api/deliverables/:id/remind + remindDeliverable() already emails, broadcasts, and logs activity for any awaiting_client client_deliverable, and GBP review responses already create such a deliverable. Only a per-target rate-limit is net-new.

verdicts.evidence: server/db/migrations/160-google-business-profile-reviews.sql google_business_reviews has review_resource_name, review_id, star_rating, comment, reviewer_display_name, reply_comment, reply_state, sync/timestamps — no url/uri/link column (grep for url|uri|link returned nothing). server/google-business-profile-reviews-store.ts maps only review_resource_name / review_id (no reviewUrl/mapsUrl). No persisted Google review/maps URL exists; capturing/constructing one is a genuine column + mapper + serialization lockstep. Because it is a DB-column addition (not a computed projection like claim 3), effort S is arguably light per the DB-column-lockstep rule, but the field is small and single-table so S is defensible.

server/routes/google-business-profile.ts:289-323 POST /review-responses/draft calls generateGbpReviewResponseDraft (AI) then upsertGbpReviewResponseDraft — AI (re)draft of a draft-status response works; PATCH /review-responses/:responseId (:325-350) edits existing draft text (manual EDIT of an existing draft, not manual CREATE); send-to-client (:353-390) is a SEPARATE two-step. No manual (no-AI) initial-draft path and no one-step draft-and-send exist. Partial is accurate; effort S stands (route + store additions, no migration).

server/state-machines.ts:172-186 GBP_REVIEW_RESPONSE_TRANSITIONS defines approved: ['publishing','cancelled'] and publish_failed: ['publishing','cancelled'] — neither lists 'draft' as a legal target, so approved→draft and publish_failed→draft reopen edges do not exist. (Cited lines were 176-180; actual span is 172-186 but the substance — no →draft reopen — is exactly correct.) Adding them plus approval-metadata clearing is net-new; effort S stands.

server/routes/local-seo.ts:270-277 deriveGbpCompletenessScore takes only { claimed, totalPhotos, attributeCount, category }; server/local-gbp.ts:115-116 the owned-listing projection captures only attributes + totalPhotos (plus claimed/category upstream). grep for regularHours|holidayHours|business_hours|serviceArea|products|questionsAndAnswers|photo cadence across local-gbp.ts, google-business-profile-client.ts, shared/types/local-seo.ts returned zero hits. Hours/holiday-hours/products/Q&A/service-area ingestion + fix flows are all absent; partial is accurate and effort L is justified (new client methods + storage + fix flows).
